etymology.description: semantic 疒(疒) (nì, "disease") + phonetic 尤(尤) (yóu) -> wart
etymology.interpretation: The character 疣(yóu, "wart") combines the semantic 疒(疒) (nì, "disease"), indicating illness or skin condition, with the phonetic 尤(尤) (yóu) to convey its pronunciation, resulting in the specific meaning of 'wart.' Warts are small, localized skin growths considered a type of minor skin disease, thus linking to the disease component.

examples.chinese: 他的手上长了一颗小疣(他的手上長了一顆小疣)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shǒu shàng zhǎngle yī kē xiǎo yóu.
examples.english: There is a small wart on his hand.

examples.chinese: 医生建议用冷冻疗法去除疣(醫生建議用冷凍療法去除疣)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiànyì yòng lěngdòng liáofǎ qùchú yóu.
examples.english: The doctor suggested using cryotherapy to remove the wart.

examples.chinese: 这种药膏可以有效地治疗疣(這種藥膏可以有效地治療疣)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng yàogāo kěyǐ yǒuxiào de zhìliáo yóu.
examples.english: This ointment can effectively treat warts.
